extraction.one_line_summary: JAKOBSEN Fabio won sprint from Ackerman's wheel after Senechal leadout, his second stage win taking points jersey
tactical_patterns: Quickstep controlled closing despite Ackerman holding points jersey, aggressive positioning over passive defending reveals Bora's sprint confidence deficit.
Senechal leadout placed Jakobsen in Ackerman's wheel from 1.5km, Quickstep prioritizing rival's slipstream over traditional train finish.
Early breakaway attempts featuring GC riders like Cavagna (17s back) immediately shut down by Sky, no tolerance for time threats.

rider_signals.JAKOBSEN Fabio: positive: Second stage win from rival's wheel confirms ability to beat Ackerman head-to-head even without perfect leadout position.
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: negative: Led sprint wearing points jersey but beaten by wheel-sitter, suggests vulnerable when forced to lead rather than counterattack.
rider_signals.CAVAGNA Remi: neutral: Attempted early move despite sitting 17s down GC, team allowing aggressive racing rather than defensive tactics.
